So the first and again, these are not in any particular order, but I will tell you that the last one is my favorite How about them apples? Okay, so the first thing I want to mention is that while you're in a fat loss phase Your calories are lower imagine that now what ends up happening is that a lot of people love to eat like eight meals of Rabbit food and that like really sucks and so what I want to tell you is that it's okay to have fewer Mondo meals because it sticks to your ribs. Okay, I'd rather not have nine salads I think I would rather have like a few like Mondo meals that stuck with me than having to deal with tuna and twigs all day So that's reason number one and the next thing is that for a lot of people Eating earlier in the day make some hungrier later in the day because of you know metabolic fluctuations in circadian timing and internal clocks and all that fancy stuff However, what I have found is that when people Including myself you go out with go without food in the morning It feels much easier to like a man. I could just go without food all day But as soon as you have like a couple of morsels and you wake up It's like man. I'm ready to go time to eat like every hour or whatever, you know Like that's how I am and I figured out that if I go without food for the first few hours I feel like I can go without food for the next few hours But if I get a meal in immediately then I want another meal in immediately Okay. Now on that note Going extended periods of time Without eating extended as well as if going extended periods of time without eating on a personal level of self-awareness on a physiological Level in your loin in your loins. I don't know your heart. I don't know some body part you feel it You're able to differentiate physiological hunger like my body needs calories versus physiological cravings which is like man cookies and pizza sound great right now and Some of you may know a man who would call that the dessert stomach or grand ma Okay, everybody has room in the dessert stomach and that's the craving part. Okay, oh That being said moving on with that in mind What you can do is that with this sort of method you can concentrate all of your food or most of your food Later in the day, especially at night when cravings are highest So a lot of people we got the tuna and twig diet going on later at night You're like man, I could really use that at that point like it doesn't even matter what sort of food you eat You're like I could just I could eat more tuna and twigs But even then if it's not on the plan go into bed hungry suck So if you can go without a little bit earlier You can have a little bit more later and go to bed with a full stomach and that makes a lot of people feel better including myself Okay Next I think I'm getting a little bit more into the fancy stuff Like little science here things is that something that is worth noting is that Your morning caffeine kick if you choose to have one is going to kick harder Okay, and this goes with any sort of pharmacy and supplementation That you decide to go with in the morning Because you don't have any food to slow the uptake of whatever you consume. Okay, this means you have higher peak levels Everything gets absorbed faster if you have sensitive tummies and maybe we consider this always asterisk But generally speaking stuff hits you harder and faster on an empty stomach And I'm sure some people from their college days understand that now Little bit further into the science here on my nose that I was gonna talk about this But I think it's cool is that you have what's called increased metabolic flexibility? Okay, especially when combined with training now boiled down This means that you can train your body through training and time-restricted feeding or intermittent fasting to be more efficient at burning fat instead of carbohydrate, especially at rest and As cool as it sounds. It's also very good for you to burn more fat when you're doing nothing You're definitely healthier that way, but it also translates into a lot of other aspects of your health and body composition as well So that is actually a legit benefit and after being adapted to time-restricted feeding After you go a few days, maybe a week with the shifted meals schedule patterns You'll notice that your productivity in the morning goes way up. Okay? And the morning is when most people are productive anyway Or the only time they have to be productive and so what happens is during these periods of fasting Your body after a few days a week or so it'll start kicking up its own output of It's natural ADHD med called epinephrine So what you'll find is that after long periods of fasting like you kind of feel high sometimes like man I feel great that's because your body's taking care of itself And so what you can do is you can engineer that sort of response to make it so that the first half of your day When you should be the most productive anyway, you can get a lot more productivity out of it Which I think is a great benefit that is not really mentioned that often and last Certainly not least definitely my favorite is that this is a sustainable method that if you choose to You can do it forever and like scouts honor That's anyway like that's like that's definitely the truth. This is something that you can do forever It's not weird socially. I mean practically speaking. It's like you just don't eat tail lunch depending on what sort of feeding pattern You have maybe maybe late lunch so you can incorporate this and it doesn't screw with your life and You can reach your body composition goals and do it in a way that's not going to drive you crazy And I hope you guys found that valuable
